name: Vuelta a Montejurra – circular desde Zúñiga por la Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro
region_intro: Mountain biking around Zúñiga features a landscape shaped by converted railway lines, offering routes through rolling hills and valleys. The region is characterized by its historical infrastructure, including tunnels and viaducts, which are integrated into many of the trails. This area provides a mix of terrain, from gentle gradients along former rail beds to more undulating paths. Best mountain bike trails around Zúñiga The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta a Montejurra – circular desde Zúñiga por la Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the historic Vasco-Navarro Railway Green Way, offering a circular journey around Montejurra. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa – Prison Bridge, Estella loop from Acedo, a moderate 28.4 miles (45.8 km) path. This route explores historical landmarks and river crossings. Local mountain bikers also love the Granada Tunnel loop from Acedo, a 20.2 miles (32.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Zúñiga is defined by converted railway lines, historical tunnels, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Zúñiga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Nearly 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zúñiga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Zúñiga?
A: There are over 65 mountain bike trails available around Zúñiga, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 7 easy, 40 moderate, and 18 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Zúñiga?
A: Mountain biking around Zúñiga is characterized by a landscape shaped by converted railway lines, featuring routes through rolling hills and valleys. You'll encounter historical infrastructure like tunnels and viaducts integrated into many trails, providing a mix of gentle gradients along former rail beds and more undulating paths.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Zúñiga?
A: Yes, Zúñiga offers 7 easy mountain bike trails. These routes often follow the former railway lines, providing smoother paths and less challenging gradients, ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Zúñiga?
A: Many routes in Zúñiga are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988096705/vuelta-a-montejurra-circular-desde-zuniga-por-la-via-verde-del-ferrocarril-vasco-navarro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta a Montejurra – circular desde Zúñiga por la Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.9-mile (43.3 km) moderate trail that loops around Montejurra.

Q: What historical features can I explore on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often incorporate the region's historical infrastructure. You can ride through old railway tunnels and over viaducts. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Tunnel loop from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; takes you through a significant tunnel, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10523804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atauri Tunnel – Vasco-Navarro Railway Bridge loop from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; features both a tunnel and a railway bridge.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves to see near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the Zúñiga area is rich in natural attractions. You can find several caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2822141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de Ollobarren Cave&lt;/a&gt;. For waterfalls, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranbaltza Dam and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from various points in the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Zúñiga?
A: The mountain bike trails around Zúñiga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the unique experience of cycling along converted railway lines, the historical tunnels, and the scenic rolling hills.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike trails in Zúñiga?
A: Yes, Zúñiga offers 18 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking more challenging terrain and technical sections. These routes typically feature steeper climbs, descents, and more rugged paths compared to the moderate or easy options.

Q: Can I find routes that explore historical landmarks beyond railway lines?
A: Absolutely. The region's history extends beyond its railway heritage. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38201242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa – Prison Bridge, Estella loop from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 28.4-mile (45.8 km) path that explores historical landmarks and includes river crossings, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike trail for a longer ride?
A: For a longer, moderate ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquijas Viaduct – Granada Tunnel loop from Acedo&lt;/a&gt;. This trail covers 27.7 miles (44.7 km) and takes you through varied terrain, including the impressive Arquijas Viaduct and the Granada Tunnel.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Zúñiga?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, but many trails remain accessible year-round.
tips:
The Zuñiga bridge is located within the route of the greenway of the Basque-Navarre railway.

A section of gravel track between Estella and Vitoria to enjoy cycling.

Built in 1925, it is one of the several tunnels found on the itinerary of the Greenway of the Vasco-Navarro Railway. The Greenway has a total length of 124 km and connects the city of Estella-Lizarra with Bergara.
Very fun circuit with jumps and cambers
